medications.indication_rationale: First-line for allergic conjunctivitis; source used olopatadine 0.1%, 1 drop each eye twice daily.
medications.dose_route_frequency: 0.1% 1 drop to each eye twice daily (as per source note)

medications.indication_rationale: For concurrent allergic rhinitis symptoms; source used cetirizine 10mg daily.
No bacterial features (watery, itchy, bilateral) — antibiotic drops are not indicated.

medications.drug_name: topical vasoconstrictor / combination decongestant eye drops (prolonged)
medications.indication_rationale: Prolonged use causes rebound hyperaemia (conjunctivitis medicamentosa); not the appropriate mainstay.

behavioural_change_actions.specific_action: Apply cold compresses to closed eyes and strictly avoid rubbing; keep drops refrigerated for added relief.
behavioural_change_actions.rationale: Cold reduces itch/swelling; rubbing ruptures mast cells and worsens histamine release.

behavioural_change_actions.specific_action: Reduce allergen exposure — keep windows closed at peak pollen, shower/change after being outdoors.
behavioural_change_actions.rationale: Removing the trigger is the definitive measure.

patient_education_points.point: This is allergic conjunctivitis — an allergic reaction in the eyes, not an infection. Vision is not at risk. Antihistamine eye drops settle it quickly, and it improves as you avoid the allergen.
Don't rub your eyes (it makes it worse), use cold compresses, and keep the drops in the fridge for extra soothing relief.

Go to urgent eye care / ED for any loss of vision, severe deep eye pain, pain moving the eye, or if it becomes one-sided and painful; call 000 for any lip/tongue/throat swelling or breathing difficulty.

follow_up_plan.what_to_reassess: Symptom control; consider referral if recurrent/severe or if any red-eye features.
follow_up_plan.trigger_for_earlier_review: Vision change, severe eye pain/photophobia, or airway swelling — urgent eye care / 000.

must_include_items: Red-eye danger-sign safety-net (vision loss, severe pain, photophobia, unilateral painful eye)
No-rubbing + cold compress + allergen avoidance advice
acceptable_alternatives: Any ocular antihistamine/mast-cell stabiliser; oral antihistamine for rhinitis
errors_of_omission: Failing to confirm preserved vision before reassurance
errors_of_commission: Prescribing antibiotic eye drops for allergic conjunctivitis
Sending a benign bilateral allergic conjunctivitis with negative red-eye/anaphylaxis screens to ED by ambulance
scoring_rubric.passing_threshold_notes: Pass requires topical antihistamine management plus both a red-eye and an anaphylaxis safety-net.
